title: Rocker-image build fails after experimenting within R instance in rootless Rocker-container
body:
### Issue Description

Hi.

I was experimenting with the renv R package in an R instance contained by a rootless rocker/r-ver:4.2.1 container. For some reason, I am not able to build the Rocker images anymore, although the container is already shutdown. I used the Podman 4.5.0 (with podman-machine-default) in Windows 10 Enterprise with WSL2 installed. It is possible that I have done something silly, or the issue is already fixed in the newer Podman. However, what is the explanation for the observed behaviour?

Here is the dockerfile I used to build the Rocker image:

```
FROM rocker/r-ver:4.2.1

WORKDIR /container

COPY container_test.r ./

CMD /bin/bash 
```

The container_test.r file contains only one line `cat("hello world")`. The image was build using:

`podman build -t testname/testcontainer:1.0 .`

And the Rocker-container instance was opened using:

`podman run --rm -ti -v .:/container testname/testcontainer:1.0`

I started an R 4.2.1 instance in the container, ran `install.packages("renv")` (likely also `library("renv")` missed in my notes) and then:

```
> init()

Welcome to renv!

It looks like this is your first time using renv. This is a one-time message,
briefly describing some of renv's functionality.

renv maintains a local cache of data on the filesystem, located at:

  - "~/.cache/R/renv"

This path can be customized: please see the documentation in `?renv::paths`.

renv will also write to files within the active project folder, including:

  - A folder 'renv' in the project directory, and
  - A lockfile called 'renv.lock' in the project directory.

In particular, projects using renv will normally use a private, per-project
R library, in which new packages will be installed. This project library is
isolated from other R libraries on your system.

In addition, renv will update files within your project directory, including:

  - .gitignore
  - .Rbuildignore
  - .Rprofile

Please read the introduction vignette with `vignette("renv")` for more information.
You can browse the package documentation online at https://rstudio.github.io/renv/.
Do you want to proceed? [y/N]:

>y

```

Because the previous run was problematic, I also tried `init(bare = TRUE)` with similar results. Then, when I tried to build another Rocker image (which shouldn't have nothing to do with the renv) the Podman failed:

```
podman build -t testname/testcontainer:1.0 .
time="2023-05-24T12:18:29+03:00" level=error msg="1 error occurred:\n\t* open C:\\some_directories\\renv\\sandbox\\R-4.2\\x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\\25ebdc09\\KernSmooth: The file cannot be accessed by the system.\n\n\n"
Error: Post "http://d/v4.5.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&httpproxy=1&identitylabel=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&isolation=1&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=testname%2Ftestcontainer%3A1.0&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&shmsize=67108864&t=testname%2Ftestcontainer%3A1.0": 
io: read/write on closed pipe
```

I believed that experimenting with the renv in a rootless Rocker container would be safe, but something obviously went wrong. As carefully as I can, I removed and reinstalled both the Podman and WSL following these recommendations, but the issue didn't go away: https://superuser.com/questions/1619233/completely-reinstall-wsl/1755410#1755410

The podman build does not work even if I change the `FROM rocker/r-ver:4.2.1` to `FROM rocker/r-ver:4.3.0` in the dockerfile, the podman still tries to access the earlier version: `… renv\\sandbox\\R-4.2\\x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\\25ebdc09\\KernSmooth`. However, I am able to build the image of this totally unrelated tutorial: https://devopscube.com/podman-tutorial-beginners/

Here are some insights from the renv experts. https://github.com/rstudio/renv/issues/1335
 
This is getting quite complicated, could somebody shed some light on what could be the issue?


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Rough steps to reproduce the issue:

1. On Windows 10 Enterprise with WSL2 installed, build the image and start a rocker/r-ver:4.2.1-container instance with Podman 4.5.0.

2. In the container, start R 4.2.1 instance and run `install.packages("renv")`. I am quite sure that `library("renv")` was also used but missed in my notes.

3. Run `init()`, possibly also `init(bare = TRUE)`.

4. Exit the container and try to rebuild the Rocker image which should now fail.

### Describe the results you received

Rebuilding the Rocker images with the Podman failed although the previous container instance was shutdown. And the problem persists even if the Podman and WSL were removed and reinstalled.

### Describe the results you expected

I expected that experimenting within an R instance contained by a rootless Rocker container is safe and shouldn't effect on the coming image builds.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

> podman run --rm -ti -v .:/container testname/testcontainer:1.0

I assume the volume mount is causing issue, especially given you get EPERMs on the mounted paths.  Mounting host paths into a rootless container has some peculiarities.  I recommend reading the following article and try the recommended solutions https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/debug-rootless-podman-mounted-volumes.

> Thanks for reaching out!
> 
> > podman run --rm -ti -v .:/container testname/testcontainer:1.0
> 
> I assume the volume mount is causing issue, especially given you get EPERMs on the mounted paths. Mounting host paths into a rootless container has some peculiarities. I recommend reading the following article and try the recommended solutions https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/debug-rootless-podman-mounted-volumes.

Thank you. Before getting familiar with the material, I would like to verify is the assumption justified? I mean it sounds odd that mounting during the `podman run` (usually done afterwards) can affect on the `podman build`, which are separate steps when dealing with the images and containers. The main problem was with the `podman build`.

Thanks, @IlmariTamminen.  I was too hasty.  The build issue you see is the following, right?
```
podman build -t testname/testcontainer:1.0 .
time="2023-05-24T12:18:29+03:00" level=error msg="1 error occurred:\n\t* open C:\\some_directories\\renv\\sandbox\\R-4.2\\x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\\25ebdc09\\KernSmooth: The file cannot be accessed by the system.\n\n\n"
Error: Post "http://d/v4.5.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&httpproxy=1&identitylabel=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&isolation=1&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=testname%2Ftestcontainer%3A1.0&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&shmsize=67108864&t=testname%2Ftestcontainer%3A1.0":
```

@flouthoc PTAL
No problem. Yes, the stuck image build is the problem.
@vrothberg Anything new regarding the issue? The issue was left closed, I was just checking that this does not get forgotten.
Sorry I missed checking this, let me re-open this.

@IlmariTamminen If its stuck on the `build` command, could you check size of your current context directory, if its too big ( podman is trying to copy it from the host to remote ) , in such case you will have to use `.containerignore` to keep files only which are needed i.e `container_test.r`

Thanks @flouthoc. Okay, the problem currently is that I have changed my computer, and "the issue is now gone with the earlier one". I also discarded the renv R package anyhow. My primary motivation was to find out have I done something silly or were there important flaws with the Podman I should be aware of in the future. If I face similar problems again, I can try your tip. It seems that the issue wasn't serious.
